summary: Sternal skin conductance correlates with hot flash occurrence because both are driven by sympathetic nervous system overactivity involving norepinephrine release and impaired thermoregulatory control. It thus acts as a valid physiological surrogate reflecting shared pathophysiology of hot flashes.
